title: excoecaria borneensis
intro: Excoecaria borneensis, also known as the Borneo Tree of Death, is a plant species native to the tropical rainforests of Borneo. The plant contains a toxic compound called phytolaccatoxin, which has medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever and pain. Excoecaria borneensis has been found to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antimalarial properties, offering various health benefits such as reduced inflammation, improved immune function, and protection against malaria. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies, including tea and extracts, which are used in traditional medicine, although caution is advised due to its toxic nature.
